reports.text:
Sakar Healthcare is a company in the pharma industry. It has three revenue segments.

1. CDMO / CMO Services : It is the company's most stable business segment. What they do is they provide end to end services to large pharma companies like product development, scale-up, regulatory support and commercial manufacturing. Some of their customers are Zydus, Emcure, Cipla, etc.

2. Own Brand Exports : This is their primary source of revenue. They manufacture generic medicines under their own brand name, and then export them to over 60+ countries.

3. Out-licensing : They share their formula of how they make a specific medicine to another company, and in return Sakar gets upfront payment, royalty, or shares profits from the company to whom they sell the formula.

Earlier, the company was heavily focused on CDMO services, but now they have started focusing on own brand exports and out-licensing, because they are high margin segment.

All the products which are manufactured in the above three revenue segments can be classified into Non-Oncology and Oncology product segments.

~Non-Oncology products are the traditional products that formed the base of the business. They are manufactured in their Changodar facility. Some examples of these products are Antibiotics, Anti-fungal and cough-syrups.

~Oncology products are the high-margin products which are manufactured in their Bavla facility. These are used to cure cancer disease.

Q4 FY26 Results :

~Revenue grew by 42% YoY to 71 cr.
~EBITDA grew by 67% YoY to 26 cr
~PAT margins expanded by 500 bps to 16%
~The oncology business continues to emerge as a key growth driver, with rising scale, expanding product reach, and increasing export opportunities supporting future momentum.
~Oncology contributed to around 38% of total revenue, while non-oncology contributed to 62%
~Oncology segment contribution almost doubled from last year.

Source - Choice Institutional Equities

CRDMO | Segment-Wise Industry Map

Key Highlights
- Five distinct segments
- Different growth profiles
- Different entry barriers
- Segment selection matters

Small-Molecule CDMO
- Largest market opportunity.
- Slowest growth segment.
- India's core strength.
- Companies: Divi's, Laurus, Neuland, Cohance.

Biologics CDMO
- Fast-growing global market.
- High technology barriers.
- India's capability gap.
- Companies: Syngene, Anthem, Aurobindo, Laurus Bio.

Peptides & GLP-1
- Fastest-growing segment.
- GLP-1 demand accelerating.
- Purification remains bottleneck.
- Companies: Divi's, Neuland, Laurus, Gland.

Discovery CRO
- Early-stage drug research.
- Sticky customer relationships.
- Funding cycle sensitive.
- Companies: Syngene, Sai Life, Anthem.

Complex & Specialty CDMO
- Highest entry barriers.
- Premium margin business.
- Qualification takes years.
- Companies: Cohance, Piramal, Gland, Concord, Blue Jet.

Segment Comparison
- Growth: Peptides > Biologics > Complex > Discovery > Small Molecules.
- Barriers: ADC/Biologics > Peptides > Complex APIs > Discovery > Commodity APIs.

Key Takeaway
- CRDMO is not a single business model. The best long-term opportunities lie in high-growth, high-barrier segments such as peptides, GLP-1, ADCs and biologics, where companies can sustain stronger pricing power and profitability.

DR REDDY'S LABORATORIES | Semaglutide Production Update

- FY27 target revised lower
- API quality issue identified
- Production resumes November

Management Update
- FY27 target cut to 6–7 million pens.
- Earlier target was 12 million pens.
- Certain API batches failed specifications.
- Issue linked to scale-up process.
- Root cause investigation continues.
- No patient safety concerns.
- Global filings remain unaffected.
- Commercial supplies delayed.
- Production to restart in November.
- Customers informed about delays.
- Some partner terms may be renegotiated.

Key Takeaway
- Dr Reddy's faces a temporary semaglutide production setback due to an API quality issue, delaying supplies and reducing FY27 output, while patient safety and regulatory approvals remain unaffected.

INDIA BANS OVER-THE-COUNTER SALE OF DRUGS CONTAINING OVER 12% ALCOHOL

The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare has notified that oral drug formulations containing more than 12% ethyl alcohol (v/v) will no longer be sold over the counter. These medicines will now require a doctor’s prescription, tightening control over products such as certain cough syrups and other alcohol-containing oral medicines to curb misuse and abuse. The notification removes their earlier exemption and brings them under stricter prescription regulations.

• 🤝 Adani Enterprises has entered a long-term partnership with France-based Dioxycle to develop and scale low-carbon chemical production in India. 

• 🏭 The collaboration will begin with India's first pilot facility to produce formic acid using captured CO₂ and renewable electricity, with plans for phased commercial-scale manufacturing. 

• ⚡ The partnership combines Dioxycle's electrochemical technology with Adani Group's renewable energy, infrastructure and execution capabilities to create sustainable, cost-competitive chemical production. 

• 🧪 Beyond formic acid, both companies will explore production of other critical low-carbon chemicals used across energy, materials, packaging and manufacturing sectors. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Marks Adani Group's strategic entry into the low-carbon chemicals business, expanding its clean-energy ecosystem and future growth opportunities.)

INDIA–AUSTRALIA | Long-Term Uranium Supply Agreement

Key Highlights
- Long-term uranium supply secured
- Supports 100 GW nuclear target
- Strengthens energy security
- Expands strategic partnership

Agreement Details
- India signed a long-term agreement with Australia to secure uranium supplies for its nuclear power plants.
- The pact enables Australian uranium exports to India exclusively for peaceful purposes under IAEA safeguards.
- The agreement comes nearly 12 years after the India–Australia Civil Nuclear Cooperation Agreement.
- India currently has 24 nuclear reactors with 8.78 GW of installed capacity.
- The government aims to increase nuclear capacity to 22 GW by FY32 and 100 GW by 2047.
- Both countries also signed agreements covering critical minerals, defence, maritime security, cyber technologies, supply chains and education.
- India and Australia will establish a Critical Minerals Corridor and a Defence Innovation Corridor to deepen strategic cooperation.
- The two nations reaffirmed their commitment to strengthening energy security, resilient supply chains and expanding bilateral trade and investment.

Key Takeaway
- The long-term uranium supply agreement marks a major milestone in India's clean energy strategy, providing fuel security for its ambitious nuclear expansion plans while also deepening India–Australia cooperation across critical minerals, energy, defence, technology and resilient supply chains, reinforcing the broader strategic partnership between the two countries.

Key Highlights
- Iron ore prices reduced
- Cuts up to ₹500/tonne

Price Revision
- NMDC has reduced iron ore prices by ₹150–500 per tonne, effective 10 July 2026.
- DR Calibrated Lump Ore (CLO, Fe 67%) is now priced at ₹5,850 per tonne.
- Iron Ore Fines (Fe 64%) are now priced at ₹4,700 per tonne.
- Prices are on an FOR Bacheli basis and exclude royalty, DMF and NMET charges.
- The price revision applies across multiple iron ore grades.

Key Takeaway
- NMDC has lowered iron ore prices for July 2026 by up to ₹500 per tonne, reflecting a softer pricing environment. The revision could benefit domestic steel producers through lower raw material costs, while potentially impacting NMDC's near-term realizations.

COCHIN SHIPYARD | Government Reduces Stake Through OFS

Key Highlights
- Government sold 4.58% stake
- OFS completed successfully
- Promoter holding reduced
- Employee reservation included

Transaction Details
- Government of India (Promoter) sold 1,20,49,170 equity shares (4.58% stake) through an Offer for Sale (OFS) conducted on 7–8 July 2026.
- Following the transaction, the Government's shareholding declined from 67.91% to 63.33%.
- An additional 26,308 equity shares were reserved for eligible employees under the OFS.

Key Takeaway
- The Government of India has reduced its stake in Cochin Shipyard through a successful OFS, increasing the company's public shareholding, while retaining a majority ownership of 63.33%.

INDIABULLS | Stake Sale Update

Key Highlights
- ACM Global Fund reduced stake
- Sold 2.03% via market
- Holding falls to 4.53%
- Promoter group unaffected

Transaction Details
- ACM Global Fund VCC sold 4.72 crore shares (2.03% stake) through the open market on 7 July 2026.
- Post-transaction, its holding declined to 10.54 crore shares (4.53%) from 15.26 crore shares (6.56%).
- The company's equity share capital remains unchanged at 232.95 crore shares, while diluted share capital stands at 235.44 crore shares, including outstanding ESOPs.
- ACM Global Fund VCC is not part of Indiabulls' Promoter or Promoter Group.

Key Takeaway
- Indiabulls witnessed a significant stake sale by public shareholder ACM Global Fund VCC, which reduced its holding from 6.56% to 4.53% through an open market transaction, while the promoter shareholding and company's capital structure remain unchanged.

INDIA-UK FTA: CAR IMPORT DUTY CUT UNDER TRQ 🚗🇮🇳🇬🇧

• 🚘 20,000 CBU ICE passenger cars allowed in the first year under the Tariff Rate Quota (TRQ)

• ⬇️ Import duty cut: • 110% → 30% for 10,000 premium cars (3000cc+ petrol / 2500cc+ diesel) • 66% → 50% for 5,000 mid-size cars (1500–3000cc petrol / 1500–2500cc diesel) • 66% → 50% for 5,000 small cars (up to 1500cc)

• 🚚 2,500 CBU commercial vehicles allowed in the first year under the quota

• ⚡ TRQ for electric, hybrid and hydrogen cars starts from the 6th year of the FTA

• 📉 Customs duty on eligible CBU ICE cars will gradually reduce to 10% by the 15th year

🟢 Impact: Positive for JLR (Tata Motors) and UK luxury car brands; competitive pressure may increase for premium car imports in India over the long term.

reports.text:
**AUROBINDO PHARMA | Citi View**

**Key Highlights**
- Sell rating maintained
- Target price raised to ₹1,270
- Valuation seen expensive
- Growth expected to slow

**Brokerage View**
- Citi maintained a **Sell** rating while raising the target price to **₹1,270** from **₹1,020**.
- The recent rally has pushed the stock to around **2 standard deviations above** its 5-year and 10-year average valuation.
- Current valuation already reflects near-term earnings benefits from **foreign exchange gains** and **PLI incentives**.
- Citi expects earnings growth to slow sharply during **FY28–FY29** due to limited pipeline visibility and a high volume base.
- Benefits from the **Lannett** acquisition may be weaker than expected, with its sales declining by around **30%** over the past year, according to IQVIA.
- Growth drivers such as **biosimilars** and the **Merck contract manufacturing business** may not fully offset pressure in the core generics business.
- The company's **PAT CAGR of 2% over five years and 6% over ten years** highlights the limitations of its volume-led growth model.
- At around **20x FY28E EPS**, the stock trades roughly **30% above** its historical average valuation.

**Key Takeaway**
- Citi **maintains a Sell rating and raises the target price to ₹1,270**, believing **Aurobindo Pharma's current valuation is stretched**, while **medium-term earnings growth could moderate** due to limited pipeline visibility and slowing momentum in its core generics business.

__Disclaimer: This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.__

reports.text:
**DIXON TECHNOLOGIES | JPMorgan View**

**Key Highlights**
- Overweight maintained
- Target price at ₹16,700
- Vivo JV approval received
- Revenue estimates upgraded

**Brokerage View**
- JPMorgan maintained an **Overweight** rating with a **₹16,700** target price.
- Dixon has received the long-awaited **Press Note 3 (PN3)** approval for its proposed JV with Vivo.
- The JV will be owned **51% by Dixon** and **49% by Vivo**.
- Vivo sells around **35 million smartphones annually** in India, with about **67% (22 million units)** expected to be manufactured through the JV.
- Management expects the JV to generate an incremental **₹30,000 crore** in revenue, supported by a higher average selling price than Dixon's existing mobile portfolio.
- Commercial operations are expected to begin in **Q3FY27**, around **60–90 days** after approval.
- JPMorgan estimates the JV will contribute **11 million units** in FY27 and **22 million units** each in FY28 and FY29.
- Brokerage increased **FY27–FY29 revenue estimates by 24–39%** and **EPS estimates by 13–18%**, with earnings growth moderated by the JV's **51:49 ownership structure**.

**Key Takeaway**
- JPMorgan **maintains an Overweight rating with a ₹16,700 target price**, viewing the **Vivo JV approval as a major growth catalyst** that could significantly expand **Dixon's smartphone manufacturing volumes and revenue trajectory** over the next few years.

__Disclaimer: This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.__

**LENSKART | JPMorgan Initiates Coverage**

**Key Highlights**
- Overweight initiated
- Target price at ₹635
- Strong long-term growth story
- Profitable scalable business

**Brokerage View**
- JPMorgan initiated coverage with an **Overweight** rating and **₹635** target price.
- Lenskart has built a scalable and profitable business addressing India's growing eyewear and eye care market.
- Technology, data analytics and customer-centric execution create strong competitive advantages.
- Store network is expected to expand to **5,600+ stores by FY30** from around **3,300+**, with network densification supporting demand growth.
- Premium products, including progressive lenses, premium frames and smart glasses, are expected to drive higher realizations.
- Growing contribution from high-retention **Gold members** and operating leverage should support earnings growth.
- JPMorgan estimates **21% revenue CAGR** and **41% EPS CAGR** during **FY26–FY30**, along with improving free cash flow and return ratios.
- Key risk remains execution challenges as the business scales further.

**Key Takeaway**
- JPMorgan **initiates coverage on Lenskart with an Overweight rating and ₹635 target price**, highlighting its **technology-led business model, expanding store network, premiumization strategy and strong earnings growth potential**, making it a compelling long-term compounding story despite premium valuations.

**Brokerage View**
- Q1FY27 results were broadly in line with expectations.
- Better-than-feared Q2 outlook may ease near-term investor concerns.
- EBIT margins could face downside risk versus consensus estimates.
- Revenue growth recovery remains gradual with no clear acceleration yet.
- Limited scope for valuation re-rating until sustained revenue growth improves.

**Key Takeaway**
- Morgan Stanley **maintains an Equal-Weight rating with a ₹2,200 target price**, stating that while **the Q2 outlook is better than feared**, **margin pressures and the lack of a meaningful acceleration in revenue growth** could limit further upside in the stock.

__Disclaimer: This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.__

**TCS | JPMorgan View After Q1 Results**

**Key Highlights**
- Overweight rating maintained
- Target price at ₹2,400
- Q1 broadly in line
- Growth recovery expected

**Brokerage View**
- Q1 revenue was in line to slightly ahead of expectations, aided by strong India business.
- International revenue remained weak as geopolitical uncertainty delayed client decision-making.
- Consumer and telecom verticals saw the biggest impact from slower spending.
- EBIT margin declined **130 bps**, mainly due to annual wage hikes, partly offset by forex gains and operational efficiencies.
- Management expects recovery from Q2, supported by AI-led deal wins and a large technology modernization backlog.
- JPMorgan expects **1% QoQ constant currency revenue growth** from Q2, with stronger momentum in H2FY27.
- Brokerage believes TCS can deliver **2.5–3% YoY constant currency growth** in FY27, potentially matching Infosys and HCLTech.
- At **12.4x valuation**, JPMorgan sees attractive value as growth is expected to accelerate.

**Key Takeaway**
- JPMorgan **maintains an Overweight rating with a ₹2,400 target price**, believing **TCS is well-positioned for a gradual recovery from Q2**, supported by **AI-led deal wins, technology transformation demand, and an improving growth outlook**, while current valuations remain attractive.

Key Highlights

JV Details
- Dixon will hold a 51% stake, while Vivo Mobile India will own 49%.
- Government of India has approved Vivo's investment, enabling incorporation of the JV.
- The joint venture will manufacture Vivo smartphones in India.
- It can also undertake electronics manufacturing for other brands.
- Initial paid-up capital will be ₹5 crore.
- Transaction is expected to be completed within one year, subject to customary approvals.

Key Takeaway
- The Dixon–Vivo joint venture marks a significant expansion in India's electronics manufacturing ecosystem, with government approval, majority ownership for Dixon, and the potential to manufacture products for multiple brands, strengthening Dixon's long-term growth prospects.

The US and Iran continue to engage in technical negotiations on nuclear issues with an eye to a diplomatic solution, a US official says, even as President Donald Trump launched a fresh round of retaliatory strikes against Tehran this week.

“The United States is still committed to finding a resolution, and technical talks continue. Iran can never possess a nuclear weapon,” the official said.

The memorandum of understanding, the official added, is “performance-based,” and Iran’s actions, including the Strait of Hormuz not being fully reopen and strikes on ships during the NATO Summit, “constitute failed performance at an unacceptable level.”

“Iran’s attacks on these innocent vessels are acts of terrorism,” the official added.

Walchandnagar Industries Latest Management interview 

~Capacity Expansion: To meet the aggressive targets set by the government (32.4 GW by 2031–32 and 100 GW by 2047), Walchandnagar has planned to raise money this year for facility expansion.

~Tripling Output: The company is setting up a brand-new, dedicated nuclear equipment manufacturing facility adjacent to its existing factory. This expansion is expected to triple their manufacturing capacity over the next 18 to 20 months 

~Targeting Private Players: This increased capacity will cater to immediate demand from public giants like NPCIL and NTPC, alongside emerging private players who have announced their nuclear entry plans 

~The deal with Australia (one of the world's leading uranium mining countries) provides major long-term confidence injection to the private sector looking to embark on nuclear energy plans.

~Long-Term De-risking: Chirag notes that India is not running out of uranium over the next decade. Instead, this deal aligns with the Viksit Bharat 2047 vision to secure fuel for the next 25 to 40 years, rather than just the immediate future

~The Scale of Opportunity: The nuclear sector represents an estimated ₹1 Lakh Crore per year opportunity, and mitigating long-term fuel risks is vital for attracting massive capital investments 

~Uranium Efficiency: Unlike thermal plants that require a continuous stream of coal, nuclear plants use loaded uranium for years on end, requiring only minimal top-ups over long cycles. Furthermore, moving toward technology like fast breeder reactors reduces the demand for new uranium even further.

~The Real Bottleneck: The primary reason for any potential delay in India’s nuclear expansion won't be fuel availability, but rather whether equipment manufacturers can keep pace with the deployment schedule

reports.text:
Equirus says Dixon secures long-awaited Vivo JV approval is a major growth catalyst

Explained below
1. Vivo shipments: 22.2mn (CY23) → 25.1mn (CY24) → 29.3mn (CY25), with market share rising 15% → 17% → 19%

2. Management previously indicated the JV could eventually manufacture ~2/3 of Vivo India's volumes = 18–20mn smartphones annually.

3. Dixon produced ~33mn smartphones in FY26. So at full ramp-up, the Vivo JV alone could lift volumes by ~55–60%, reinforcing its leadership in India's smartphone EMS market.

4. FY27 guidance of ~₹56,000cr revenue & 32–33mn smartphone volumes excluded any Vivo contribution.

5. Equirus expects production to begin in 3QFY27, with 8–10mn incremental units in FY27 & a 18–20mn annualized run-rate as the JV scales.

Bottom line: The Vivo JV is a meaningful incremental growth driver beyond Dixon's existing FY27 guidance.

TCS Q1FY27 Results:-

➤ LTM Attrition (IT Services) 13.6%

➤ Won an US$800 million AI-led mega deal with SKF

➤ Signed a multi-million-dollar strategic partnership with ServiceNow

➤ Secured a multi-million-dollar AI transformation deal with a Europe-based Fortune Global 50 company

➤ Continued strong momentum across AI, software engineering, modernization, cybersecurity, sovereign cloud and platform transformation

➤ Expanded ecosystem partnerships with Anthropic and Mistral AI to strengthen AI offerings

➤ Management Commentary
✓ Q1 FY27 reflected continued growth despite macro and geopolitical challenges

✓ Strong US$9.5 billion TCV, including the marquee US$800 million SKF AI transformation deal

✓ Annualized AI revenue reached US$2.6 billion

✓ Growing client investments in AI, modernization, cybersecurity, sovereign cloud and platform simplification, along with better deal conversion and client mining, position TCS for sustained growth

✓ Strong growth across multiple service lines during the quarter

✓ Won several AI-led transformation deals focused on AI optimization and innovation

✓ Expanded capabilities in AI-powered IT operations, software engineering, modernization, AI-first process redesign, SaaS implementation and Autonomous GBS

✓ Strengthened the AI ecosystem through strategic partnerships with Anthropic and Mistral

✓ Implemented annual wage hikes in Q1

✓ Continued investments in partnerships and AI capabilities to enhance long-term competitiveness

✓ Maintained disciplined execution alongside industry-leading profitability and return ratios

✓ Completed annual salary increments globally and aligned compensation with the new India Labour Code

✓ Continued investments in AI infrastructure and next-generation skill development to build a future-ready workforce while fostering an inclusive and trusted workplace
INDIA’S FOOD SERVICES MARKET PROJECTED TO HIT USD 150 BILLION BY FY31, ONLINE SHARE TO CLIMB TO 18%

TCS Q1 : COO Says Q1 was characterized by strong growth across several services. 

We won multiple Al-led transformation deals with our dual commitment to Al-led optimization as well as innovation-led outcomes. 

These wins validate our approach to Al-led efficient ITOps, accelerated Software Engineering and Modernization, Al-first process redesign and implementation of SaaS solutions and Autonomous GBS. 

We signed strategic partnerships with Anthropic and Mistral expanding our Al ecosystem".

TCS CEO Says Q1 FY27 reflects continued growth momentum and the strength of our strategic positioning, despite geopolitical and macro-economic headwinds. 

We delivered a strong order book of $9.5 billion, including a marquee Al-led transformation deal with SKF, while continuing to add clients across key revenue bands and scaling our Al business to a $2.6 billion annualized revenue run rate. 

As customers accelerate investments in Al, modernization, cybersecurity, sovereign cloud and platform simplification, our strong deal conversion, improving client mining and expanding ecosystem partnerships position TCS well to translate opportunity into sustained growth".

• Global salary hikes completed.

reports.text:
Real Estate Pre-sales growth over last few years

Top 4 developers combined Pre-sales increased from ₹213 Bn in FY21 to ₹1,049 Bn in FY26 (5x growth)

Branded developers are capturing a larger share of the market

Most real estate companies follow the project completion method, which basically means revenue can be recognized only when the project is delivered

So the current P&L will not fully reflect the current business position

As projects start getting completed, Pre-Sales will start flowing into the P&L

A lot of pre-sales have already happened over the last 5 years, but the revenue recognition will come as projects get delivered

Unrecognized revenue for many developers is sitting at record levels

The interesting part is that many of these projects are in the luxury / premium category, where embedded margins are also higher

As deliveries pick up over the next few years, P&L of real estate companies will start looking robust

This delivery cycle can also benefit many building material players, as project execution & handovers increase

The Company has secured five new orders across MEP, civil and electrical works, strengthening its execution pipeline across footwear manufacturing, industrial facilities, consumer products and food processing sectors.

* Additional confirmed orders worth approximately ₹75.52 Cr (excluding GST)
* Revised FY27 confirmed order book at approximately ₹915.74 Cr (excluding GST), up from ₹840.22 Cr (excluding GST)

* Execution Timeline: Before December 2026
* Subsidiary of Taiwan based Pou Chen Corporation Industrial Group, the world’s largest branded athletic and casual footwear manufacturer

2. Grand Atlantia Panapakkam SEZ Developers Private Limited

* MEP works for the proposed building at SIPCOT Park, Panapakkam, Ranipet
* Order Value: ₹25.55 Cr, including GST
* Execution Timeline: Before October 2026
* Part of Taiwan based Hong Fu Industrial Group, which is investing approximately ₹1,500 Cr in a mega non leather footwear manufacturing facility in Ranipet

3. Anabond Limited

* Civil works at APIC Industrial Park, Andhra Pradesh
* Order Value: ₹19.48 Cr, including GST
* Execution Timeline: Before January 2027

* New project site establishment civil works in Bijapur, Karnataka
* Order Value: ₹1.22 Cr, including GST
* Execution Timeline: Before October 2026

* Electrical works in Karaikal, Pondicherry
* Order Value: ₹2.26 Cr, including GST

**NOMURA: Cuts Q1FY27 Estimates for OMCs**

• Nomura expects **HPCL, BPCL and IOCL** to report **Q1FY27 EBITDA losses** of around **₹13,900 crore**, **₹15,800 crore** and **₹17,300 crore**, respectively.

• The losses are driven by **higher crude oil prices**, as OMCs were unable to fully pass on increased fuel costs to consumers.

• **Retail marketing margins** are estimated to decline to a **loss of ₹20–21/litre** in Q1FY27, compared with a **loss of around ₹5/litre** in Q4FY26.

• **Higher Gross Refining Margins (GRMs)** are expected to partially offset the marketing losses.

• **Higher refinery transfer prices** and **rupee depreciation** also impacted profitability.

**INCRED | DIAMOND POWER INFRASTRUCTURE**

Initiated coverage with a **High-Conviction ADD** rating and a **target price of ₹300**.

The brokerage expects **plant utilisation** to increase from **~25% currently to ~60% by FY28**, driving operating leverage.

InCred forecasts **~5x revenue growth** over the next **three years**, with **EBITDA expected to grow at ~73% CAGR**.

Growth is expected to be supported by strong demand for **MV/EHV cables, underground cabling, data centres**, and **power infrastructure**.

The brokerage also noted that the **NCLT overhang has been resolved**, with the balance sheet expected to strengthen and **minimal capex** required over the next two years.

**Key Takeaway**
InCred sees Diamond Power as a high-growth play, driven by improving utilisation, strong industry tailwinds, and a healthier balance sheet.

📊 MS INDIA STRATEGY | Q1 FY27 Earnings Preview

🔹 Revenue growth is expected to remain strong in Q1 FY27.
🔹 Margins may face pressure due to higher input costs.
🔹 All 10 sectors are expected to report positive revenue growth.
🔹 Consumer Discretionary, Communication Services & Industrials are likely to lead revenue growth.
🔹 Earnings growth is expected to be driven by Communication Services, Materials & Financials.
🔹 Energy and Industrials may report earnings declines.
🔹 Bharti Airtel and Axis Bank are expected to contribute the most to Sensex earnings, while IndiGo may be the weakest performer.

📌 Key Takeaway
India's earnings outlook remains positive, supported by policy stimulus and improving business activity despite margin pressures.

📊 JEFFERIES INDIA STRATEGY | Q1 FY27 Earnings Preview

🔹 Revenue growth is expected to reach a 13-quarter high.
🔹 Strong economic activity and rising Nominal GDP to support growth.
🔹 Higher energy and input costs may pressure margins.
🔹 Earnings (excluding O&G & Metals) expected to grow ~12% YoY.
🔹 Lending Financials, Non-lending Financials & Telecom may report 15%+ YoY earnings growth.
🔹 Cement, Pharma, IndiGo & Chemicals are expected to see earnings decline.

📌 Key Takeaway
Revenue momentum remains strong, while earnings growth is expected to stay resilient despite cost pressures.

Maintained **Buy** rating with a **target price of ₹1,000**.

The brokerage highlighted the company's plan to expand its Ayurveda healthcare network from **~2,900 beds to 7,000–10,000 beds** over the next **3–5 years**.

Management targets **₹3,000 crore revenue**, a **50:50 Services-Products mix**, and **4–5x PAT growth** through hospital expansion, OTC products, and wellness initiatives.

**CITI | KALYAN JEWELLERS**

Maintained **Buy** rating with an unchanged **target price of ₹750**.

India revenue grew **38% YoY**, slightly below Citi's **43%** estimate, while consolidated revenue also increased **38% YoY**.

The brokerage highlighted healthy **same-store sales growth** across key markets, although Middle East footfalls were impacted by April's geopolitical tensions.

Citi expects the company's **franchise-led expansion** and **asset-light model** to support future revenue growth, deleveraging, and improved ROCE.

**Key Takeaway**
Citi remains positive on Kalyan Jewellers, supported by healthy domestic demand and a scalable franchise-led growth strategy.

**KOTAK SECURITIES | THERMAX**

The brokerage noted that international and new businesses are beginning to scale up, while previously underperforming businesses are witnessing margin improvement.

**Key Takeaway**
Kotak acknowledges improving business fundamentals but continues to maintain a **Sell** rating despite the higher target price.

**MOSL | MARICO**

Maintained **Buy** rating and raised the **target price to ₹1,000** from **₹950**.

The brokerage expects Marico's growth outperformance to continue in FY27, supported by a rebound in **Parachute volumes** and sustained momentum in **VAHO**.

Growth is also expected from the **Food** and **Premium Personal Care** portfolios, while the international business focuses on premiumisation and diversification.

Operating margins are likely to improve as input cost pressures ease.

MOSL projects a **FY26–FY28 CAGR** of **13% Revenue**, **21% EBITDA**, and **18% APAT**.

**Key Takeaway**
MOSL continues to view Marico as a top pick, expecting strong earnings growth and sustained premium valuations.

Swing trading | Positional Trading
@Wealthcreator7

**ELARA CAPITAL | INTERGLOBE AVIATION (INDIGO)**

Maintained **Buy** rating.

Elara expects **Q1FY27 PAT of ₹263 crore**, compared with **₹218 crore** in Q1FY26.

Strong fare environment is expected to support earnings, with **domestic airfares up 19% YoY** and **international airfares up 40% YoY**, despite higher fuel costs.

Domestic aviation traffic is estimated to decline **2% YoY**, while the **passenger load factor** is expected at **83.4%**.

**Key Takeaway**
Elara expects structural supply constraints to keep airfares firm, supporting IndiGo's earnings despite near-term traffic softness.

Goldman Sachs expects sustained mid-teens billings growth to support earnings upgrades and further stock re-rating.

@Fundamental3

**MORGAN STANLEY | INDIA STRATEGY – Q1 FY27 Preview**

Revenue growth momentum is expected to remain healthy in Q1, although **higher input costs** may pressure margins.

Morgan Stanley believes India remains in a strong earnings growth phase, supported by **policy stimulus** and improving economic activity.

All **10 sectors** are expected to report positive revenue growth, led by **Consumer Discretionary, Communication Services** and **Industrials**.

Earnings growth is expected to be driven by **Communication Services, Materials** and **Financials**, while **Energy** and **Industrials** may see earnings decline.

**Bharti Airtel** and **Axis Bank** are expected to be the largest contributors to Sensex earnings, while **IndiGo** may be the weakest performer.

**Key Takeaway**
Morgan Stanley expects broad-based revenue growth in Q1, with financials and communication services leading earnings despite margin pressures.

**CLSA | PERSISTENT SYSTEMS**

Maintained **High Conviction Outperform** rating with a **target price of ₹5,659**.

CLSA views the **Nagarro acquisition** as a strong strategic fit and believes the rationale presented by Persistent supports the transaction.

The combined entity targets **mid-teens organic revenue growth** and **16–17% EBIT margins** over the next **6–8 quarters**.

The brokerage expects the acquisition to deliver around **4% EPS accretion** to FY28 proforma estimates.

**Key Takeaway**
CLSA remains bullish on Persistent, expecting the Nagarro acquisition to strengthen growth, profitability, and earnings.

**JEFFERIES | INDIA STRATEGY – Q1 FY27 Preview**

Revenue growth is expected to reach a **13-quarter high**, supported by resilient economic activity and rising **Nominal GDP**.

Higher **energy and input costs** may pressure margins for select domestic companies.

Earnings excluding **Oil & Gas** and **Metals** are expected to grow around **12% YoY**.

**Lending Financials, Non-lending Financials** and **Telecom** are expected to deliver **15%+ YoY** earnings growth.

**Cement, Pharma, IndiGo** and **Chemicals** are likely to report a YoY decline in earnings.

**Key Takeaway**
Jefferies expects strong revenue momentum in Q1, with financials and telecom leading earnings growth despite margin pressures.

**GOLDMAN SACHS | L&T**

Maintained **Buy** rating with a **target price of ₹4,500**.

The **Middle East** has become the key driver of L&T's international business, contributing **40% of the FY26 order book** and **36% of FY26 order inflows**.

The company is well-positioned to benefit from investments in **renewables, clean hydrogen**, and resilience-led infrastructure spending.

Multi-year project visibility is expected to reduce earnings cyclicality and support long-term growth.

**Key Takeaway**
Goldman Sachs remains positive on L&T, citing strong Middle East exposure and long-term infrastructure opportunities.

**MANKIND PHARMA | InCred View**

**Key Highlights**
- ADD rating initiated
- Target Price at ₹2,812
- Strategic business transformation
- Focus on R&D and niche therapies
- BSV acquisition strengthens portfolio
- Long-term growth opportunity

**Brokerage Updates**
- **Rating:** **InCred** initiated coverage with an **ADD** rating and a **Target Price of ₹2,812**.
- **Business Transformation:** The brokerage believes Mankind Pharma is **transitioning from a volume- and distribution-led generics model** towards an **R&D-driven, niche therapies-focused business**, reflecting the maturity of its legacy growth model.
- **Strategic Acquisition:** The **BSV acquisition** provides a strong entry into the **specialty biologics** segment, enhancing the company's presence in high-value therapies.
- **Growth Strategy:** InCred noted that **recent growth has been driven more by portfolio acquisitions than organic volume expansion**, with the benefits of the transformation expected to materialize gradually over time.

**Key Takeaway**
- InCred is **constructive on Mankind Pharma's long-term prospects**, believing its **strategic shift toward specialty therapies, R&D and biologics** positions the company for sustainable growth, although the full benefits are expected to unfold gradually.

__Disclaimer: This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.__

**Stock Stats | Stock info | Infographics
****@Stockinfo333**

**JSPL | Brokerage View**

**Key Highlights**
- Sell rating maintained
- Target Price at ₹980
- Volume growth estimated at 11%
- EBITDA/tonne may peak
- Earnings downgrade risk
- Valuation remains expensive

**Brokerage Updates**
- **Rating:** **Sell** with a **Target Price of ₹980**.
- **Volume Growth:** Estimated **Q1 FY27 volume growth** of **11% YoY**, indicating potential downside risk to JSPL's FY27 sales volume target of **10.5–11 million tonnes**, which implies **21–26% YoY growth**.
- **Profitability:** A **sharp correction in long steel prices** (down **over 15% since April 2026**) is likely to result in **EBITDA per tonne peaking in Q1 FY27**.
- **Earnings Outlook:** The brokerage expects **consensus EBITDA estimates to be revised downward** as steel realizations soften.
- **Valuation:** Based on **spot EBITDA per tonne** and expanded production volumes, **JSPL is trading at around 12x EV/EBITDA**, which the brokerage considers demanding.

**Key Takeaway**
- The brokerage remains **cautious on JSPL**, citing **slowing profitability, downside risk to volume guidance and expensive valuations**, despite continued capacity expansion.

__Disclaimer: This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.__

**TRENT | Citi View**

**Key Highlights**
- Sell rating maintained
- Target Price at ₹2,733
- Revenue missed estimates
- Revenue productivity remained weak
- Store additions ahead of estimates
- Competitive pressures persist

**Brokerage Updates**
- **Rating:** **Citi** maintained its **Sell** rating with a **Target Price of ₹2,733**.
- **Revenue:** **Q1 FY27 standalone revenue** grew **19% YoY**, below Citi's **23% estimate**.
- **Revenue Productivity:** **Average revenue per square foot** (assuming similar new-store size as TTM) **declined 12.2% YoY**, indicating continued weakness despite a favourable base.
- **Store Expansion:** **Westside** added **1 net store** and **Zudio** added **19 net stores** during the quarter, ahead of Citi's expectations of **0** and **6** stores, respectively.
- **Seasonality:** Citi noted that **Q1 is typically the weakest quarter for store additions**, making the expansion pace less of a concern.
- **Outlook:** The brokerage remains **cautious** due to **weak revenue per square foot, rising competition, store cannibalization and continued expansion into Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities**.

**Key Takeaway**
- Citi maintains a **negative stance on Trent**, believing that **declining store productivity, intensifying competition and expansion-related challenges** could weigh on future growth despite continued store additions.

__Disclaimer: This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.__

**TRENT | Bernstein View**

**Key Highlights**
- Outperform rating maintained
- Target Price at ₹3,500
- Revenue slightly missed estimates
- Westside additions remained weak
- Zudio expansion stayed healthy
- FY27 outlook unchanged

**Brokerage Updates**
- **Rating:** **Bernstein** maintained its **Outperform (O-P)** rating with a **Target Price of ₹3,500**.
- **Revenue:** **Q1 FY27 standalone revenue** grew **19% YoY**, slightly below Bernstein's **20.5% estimate**.
- **Growth:** Revenue growth was **below expectations**, reflecting a softer start to the quarter.
- **Store Expansion:** **Westside** added **1 net store**, while **Zudio** added **19 net stores** during the quarter.
- **Store Additions:** Overall additions were **below Bernstein's expectations** and **lower than Q1 FY25**.
- **Outlook:** Bernstein noted that **Q1 is typically the slowest quarter for store additions** and therefore remains **not concerned about Trent achieving its accelerated FY27 store expansion guidance**.

**Key Takeaway**
- Bernstein continues to **favour Trent**, believing the **slight revenue miss and slower store additions are temporary**, while maintaining confidence in the company's **FY27 expansion plans and long-term growth trajectory**.

__Disclaimer: This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.__

**TRENT | Morgan Stanley View**

**Key Highlights**
- Overweight rating maintained
- Target Price at ₹3,151
- Revenue broadly in line
- Store additions moderated
- Margin improvement expected
- Near-term stock weakness possible

**Brokerage Updates**
- **Rating:** **Morgan Stanley (MS)** maintained its **Overweight (OW)** rating with a **Target Price of ₹3,151**.
- **Revenue:** **Q1 FY27 standalone revenue (excluding GST)** grew **19% YoY**, slightly below Morgan Stanley's **21% estimate**, and broadly in line with **20% growth in Q4 FY26**.
- **Store Expansion:** The company added **19 net Zudio stores** and **1 Westside store** during the quarter, reflecting a **slower expansion pace** compared to the strong additions in Q4 FY26.
- **Margins:** Morgan Stanley expects **Q1 FY27 standalone EBITDA margin** to improve **100 bps YoY** to **18.5%**, compared with **18.6% in Q4 FY26**.
- **Stock View:** With the stock having **rallied around 20% over the past month**, Morgan Stanley believes the **slightly weaker-than-expected revenue print could lead to near-term profit booking**.

**Key Takeaway**
- Morgan Stanley remains **constructive on Trent's long-term outlook**, supported by **healthy revenue growth and margin expansion**, but expects **near-term stock performance to remain subdued** following the recent rally and slightly softer Q1 business update.

__Disclaimer: This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.__

**JUBILANT FOODWORKS | CLSA View**

**Key Highlights**
- Outperform rating maintained
- Target Price at ₹520
- Revenue beat estimates
- Domino's LFL exceeded expectations
- Strong standalone performance
- Eurasia business remained weak

**Brokerage Updates**
- **Rating:** **CLSA** maintained its **Outperform (O-P)** rating with a **Target Price of ₹520**.
- **Standalone Revenue:** **Q1 FY27 standalone sales** grew **9.2% YoY** to **₹1,849 Cr**, outperforming CLSA's **5% growth estimate** and broadly in line with consensus expectations.
- **Domino's India:** Reported **2.5% Like-for-Like (LFL) sales growth**, slightly ahead of CLSA's expectations.
- **Consolidated Revenue:** Increased **14.1% YoY**, reflecting continued growth across the group.
- **Eurasia Business:** Reported **-1.3% LFL growth**, indicating continued weakness in the international business.

**Key Takeaway**
- CLSA remains **positive on Jubilant FoodWorks**, supported by **better-than-expected India business performance, improving same-store sales at Domino's India and healthy revenue growth**, although the **Eurasia business remains a key area to watch**.

__Disclaimer: This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.__

**JUBILANT FOODWORKS | Morgan Stanley View**

**Key Highlights**
- Equal-Weight rating maintained
- Target Price at ₹486
- India business met expectations
- LFL growth improved sequentially
- Store expansion continued
- Margins remain key monitorable

**Brokerage Updates**
- **Rating:** **Morgan Stanley (MS)** maintained its **Equal-Weight (EW)** rating with a **Target Price of ₹486**.
- **India Business:** Standalone **Q1 FY27 revenue** grew **9.2% YoY**, broadly in line with Morgan Stanley's **9% estimate**.
- **Same-Store Sales:** **Like-for-Like (LFL) growth** improved to **2.5%**, compared with **0.2% in Q4 FY26**, but was slightly below the brokerage's **3% estimate**.
- **Store Expansion:** The company added **58 new stores** during the quarter, ahead of Morgan Stanley's expectation of **50 stores**, resulting in a **12% YoY increase in store count**.
- **Consolidated Revenue:** Increased **14.1% YoY**, below Morgan Stanley's **18% growth estimate**.
- **Outlook:** Morgan Stanley noted that the **sequential improvement in India growth** is in line with management guidance, but **LFL growth remains below the company's long-term target of 5–7%**.
- **Margins:** The brokerage expects **standalone EBITDA margin** to moderate to **20%** in Q1 FY27 from **20.5% in Q4 FY26**, citing **inflationary pressures** as the key monitorable.

**Key Takeaway**
- Morgan Stanley remains **neutral on Jubilant FoodWorks**, acknowledging the **improving sales trajectory and healthy store expansion**, while highlighting that **margin pressure and the pace of same-store sales recovery** will be the key factors to watch in the upcoming results.

__Disclaimer: This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.__

**TITAN COMPANY | Citi View**

**Key Highlights**
- Buy rating maintained
- Target Price at ₹5,075
- Jewellery growth beat estimates
- Healthy festive demand
- Strong retail expansion
- Broad-based business growth

**Brokerage Updates**
- **Rating:** **Citi** maintained its **Buy** rating with a **Target Price of ₹5,075**.
- **Jewellery Performance:** **Domestic jewellery (excluding bullion)** reported **39% YoY growth**, ahead of **Citi's estimate of 35%**, despite **gold prices rising around 59% YoY**.
- **Growth Drivers:** Management attributed the strong performance to **healthy festive demand**, including **Akshaya Tritiya**, during the quarter.
- **Consumer Trends:** **Buyer growth** remained in the **early double digits**, while the **average ticket size** increased by **high double digits**.
- **Product Mix:** Both **plain gold** and **studded jewellery** businesses grew in the **mid-30% range**, indicating a **stable studded jewellery mix** (excluding gold coins).
- **Retail Expansion:** **Tanishq, Mia, Zoya and beYon** added **22 new stores** across India during the quarter.
- **Other Businesses:** **CaratLane** delivered **42% YoY growth**, while **Watches & Wearables (+23%)**, **EyeCare (+23%)** and **Emerging Businesses (+19%)** also reported strong growth.

**Key Takeaway**
- Citi remains **positive on Titan**, supported by **better-than-expected jewellery growth, healthy consumer demand, continued retail expansion and strong performance across all major business segments**, reinforcing confidence in its long-term growth outlook.

- Leading ODM/OEM manufacturer

**Business Updates**
- PG Electroplast has evolved from a **plastic moulding company** into a leading **ODM/OEM manufacturer** for **room air conditioners, washing machines, TVs and consumer durables**, serving **70+ Indian and global brands**.
- Operates **12 manufacturing plants** and is currently the **second-largest ODM player** in **room air conditioners and washing machines**.
- Backward integration, including in-house manufacturing of key RAC components and an upcoming **compressor facility**, is expected to improve margins and cost competitiveness.
- Product business contribution increased to **~76% in FY26**, compared with **23% in FY20**, driven by rapid growth in the RAC segment.
- Despite a weak summer season, the company reported **FY26 revenue of ₹5,288 crore**, supported by strong execution and customer diversification.
- Revenue is estimated to grow at **~26% CAGR** during **FY26–FY28E**, while **PAT is projected to grow at ~56% CAGR**.

**Key Takeaway**
- PG Electroplast is **moving up the value chain** through backward integration and capacity expansion, positioning itself to benefit from the **outsourcing trend in India's white goods manufacturing**.

****@Fundamental3**

**BANKING SECTOR | Q1FY27 Business Update (Motilal Oswal)**

**Key Highlights**
- Credit growth accelerates to 16.3%
- Deposit growth improves to 12.3%
- Regional banks lead performance
- LDR strengthens across the sector

**Sector Snapshot**
- **26 of 33** listed universal banks have reported Q1FY27 business updates.
- Aggregate **credit growth** accelerated to **16.3% YoY** (vs. 13.9% in Q4FY26).
- Aggregate **deposit growth** improved to **12.3% YoY** (vs. 11.3% in Q4FY26).
- **Loan-to-Deposit Ratio (LDR)** increased by **114 bps QoQ** to **86.1%**.
- **CASA balances** remained stable at **11.0% YoY**.

**Cohort-wise Performance**

**Large Private Banks**
- Credit growth improved to **16.2% YoY**.
- Deposit growth strengthened to **15.3% YoY**.
- LDR improved by **97 bps QoQ**.
- Growth led by **Axis Bank**, while **HDFC Bank** showed recovery.

**New Age Private Banks**
- Credit growth accelerated to **13.5% YoY**.
- Deposit growth improved to **11.7% YoY**.
- LDR increased by **57 bps QoQ**.
- Growth supported by **AU Small Finance Bank, IDFC First Bank, RBL Bank** and **Yes Bank**.

**Public Sector Banks**
- Credit growth improved to **16.7% YoY**.
- Deposit growth rose to **10.7% YoY**.
- LDR expanded by **105 bps QoQ**.
- Strong momentum across major PSU banks.

**Regional Banks**
- Strongest-performing cohort with **19.3% YoY credit growth**.
- Deposit growth stood at **13.7% YoY**.
- LDR improved by **111 bps QoQ**.
- Healthy growth reported by **Karur Vysya Bank, South Indian Bank, CSB Bank, Tamilnad Mercantile Bank** and **Dhanlaxmi Bank**.

**Key Takeaway**
- Motilal Oswal expects **Q1FY27 to reflect broad-based strength across the banking sector**, with **faster credit growth, improving deposit mobilisation and stable CASA trends**. **Regional banks continue to outperform**, while **large private banks are witnessing improving business momentum**, supporting a constructive outlook for upcoming quarterly earnings.

**CLSA on Industrials**

Indian government has granted a two-year exemption to four Chinese electrical equipment manufacturers with manufacturing units in India, allowing them to participate in government/CPSE tenders.

More important is that this exemption was granted at request of Ministry of Power, validating case led by complaints from transmission utilities struggling with price inflation on controlled supply chains

Do not see this move impacting near-term volume growth, but increased competition (ex-HVDC converters) is likely to weigh on pricing power & margins of domestic T&D equipment cos such as Hitachi, GE Vernova T&D, BHEL (U-PF) & CG Power

Investors should be wary of lofty valuations (PE of 50-90x) on historic peak high margins led by ‘Make in India” driven shield but now govt seems to have taken cognisance of excessive margins being made by transformer sector.

**Stock Stats | Stock info | Infographics
****@Stockinfo333**

**Macquarie on Industrials**

As per industry sources, government is set to exempt 4 Chinese players (detailed below), having manufacturing units in India, from the restriction imposed in February 2023 for critical power projects.

This exemption is valid for a period of two years (till June 2028)

Most of grid companies are in midst of capacity expansion and are likely to add capacity over the next 2-3 year, which is the validity of this exemption.

Should this exemption be to extend in future, long-term negative impact on margins of the incumbents, especially the 765kV GIS players cannot be ruled out

Barring TBEA, most of other companies have sub-scale operations in India & are likely to take time to ramp up

Believe this exemption is to address current shortage of certain of EHV grid equipment & unlikely to allow large scale Chinese imports in sensitive areas of grid infrastructure.

Jefferies on Industrials

Media reports highlight that government has approved four Chinese companies (TBEA Energy, Nanjing Electric, New Northeast Electric, Taikai Electric) to supply equipment for government projects only from their Indian manufacturing facilities.

Import restrictions still remain.

Even with supply from these facilities, believe demand-supply mismatch remains.

Remain positive on Hitachi Energy and Siemens Energy and see the correction as a buying opportunity

**Nomura on Industrials**

Overreaction to narrow exemption

Order explicitly states it creates no precedent

Market players read it as a competitive threat & sold off listed power-equipment stocks such as - GE Vernova T&D India (Buy), CG Power (Buy), Hitachi Energy Not rated) & Siemens Energy Not rated)

Exempted players won 9% of transmission-linked PGCIL tenders over FY09-20

Uneven capacity readiness across four entities: only one appears to be running near-optimal utilization

Exemption is explicitly sunset-bound, & grid infrastructure sits squarely in national-security bucket

Two-year window is too short to underwrite meaningful capacity expansion in India

**POWER T&D | Brokerage Views After Friday's Correction**

**Key Highlights**
- Brokerages divided on correction
- Chinese exemption valid for 2 years
- Demand outlook remains strong
- Margins remain key monitorable

**Brokerage Views**
- **Nomura:** Calls the sell-off an overreaction; exemption is narrow, temporary and does not set a precedent.
- **Jefferies:** Sees the correction as a buying opportunity; remains positive on Hitachi Energy and Siemens Energy.
- **Macquarie:** Exemption addresses current equipment shortages but could pressure margins if extended beyond two years.
- **CLSA:** Expects higher competition to weigh on pricing power and margins; advises caution on rich valuations.

**Key Takeaway**
- While **Nomura, Jefferies and Macquarie** believe the **two-year exemption for select Chinese manufacturers is unlikely to derail India's long-term power transmission capex cycle**, **CLSA** cautions that **higher competition could compress margins for domestic T&D equipment companies**, making **execution and valuations key factors to watch**.
